rankings.name: Kaid D'authie
key_positive_factors: Co-top-rated on RPR (169) following a dominant Grade 1 victory at the Dublin Racing Festival
Exceptional jumping technique and attitude under pressure, ideal for the Brown Advisory
Strong upward trend in performance, winning three of four starts over fences
key_risks: Must prove stamina over this new 3m1f trip
Deep ground at DRF may have flattered his margin of victory

rankings.name: Romeo Coolio
key_positive_factors: Unbeaten 4-4 over fences and arguably the most naturally talented horse in the field
Elite Festival pedigree (2nd in Bumper, 3rd in Supreme) showing high course suitability
Matches the field-best RPR of 169 despite appearing lazy in his races
key_risks: Significant stamina query stepping up from 2m1f to 3m1f
First-time hood and lazy racing style suggest concerns about his concentration and energy distribution

rankings.name: Final Demand
key_positive_factors: Impressive winner of first three chase starts before a respectable 3rd at the DRF
High-quality Top Speed (154) indicates he can handle a strongly run Grade 1
Yard has an exceptional record in this race with improving novice chasers
key_risks: First-time tongue-tie suggests a potential breathing issue was detected after his last run
Needs to reverse a 12l deficit with Kaid D'authie

rankings.name: Koktail Divin
key_positive_factors: Stunning 21-length victory at Leopardstown over Christmas indicates massive potential
Highly competitive RPR of 166 puts him right in the mix with the favorites
Strong jumping profile and has already beaten some smart rivals this season
key_risks: Skipping a handicap for this Grade 1 is a bold move that may expose his lack of experience
Has yet to face a test as stern as the Cheltenham hill at this distance

rankings.name: Oscars Brother
key_positive_factors: Arrives on a hat-trick after winning two Grade 2 races in convincing fashion
Proven stamina over 3m and thrives on soft/heavy ground
Official Rating and RPR (164) are trending sharply upwards
key_risks: Representing a smaller yard against the Mullins/Elliott juggernauts
May lack the tactical speed of some rivals if the pace is moderate

rankings.name: Western Fold
key_positive_factors: Excellent 2nd to Kaid D'authie at the DRF, proving he belongs at this level
Galway Plate winner with high-level consistency (111132 form)
Solid RPR of 165 and proven ability to handle large fields and high pressure
key_risks: Potentially exposed as slightly below the very elite Grade 1 tier
Often finds one or two too good in the closing stages of top-level races

rankings.name: Wendigo
key_positive_factors: Considered the strongest British challenger after an easy 3m win at Ayr
Excellent course form (5th in Albert Bartlett) and a high TS of 152
Progressive profile over fences (2131) and stays the trip well
key_risks: Form in British novice races may not be as strong as the Irish Grade 1 lines
Needs to find another 8-10lbs of improvement to win

rankings.name: Kitzbuhel
key_positive_factors: Winner of the Grade 1 Kauto Star on Boxing Day, showing high-class ability
Possesses a strong Top Speed of 154 and a peak RPR of 164
key_risks: Pronounced tendency to jump to the right is a major disadvantage on this left-handed track
Unseated last time and looks to have been surpassed by several stablemates

rankings.name: Thomas Mor
key_positive_factors: Consistent performer who was a good 2nd in the Kauto Star
High Top Speed (152) and proven stamina over the distance
key_risks: Missed a key prep race due to an abscess
Likely to struggle for pace against the top-tier Irish novices

rankings.name: Argento Boy
key_positive_factors: Grade 3 winner at Naas who will certainly stay the marathon distance
Trainer W.P. Mullins' second-string runners often outperform their odds
key_risks: Bulk of his form has come on heavy ground; may find this surface too quick
RPR of 156 suggests he is significantly behind the principles

rankings.name: Salver
key_positive_factors: Dual Grade 2 winner who is tough and reliable
Solid RPR of 161 and Top Speed of 150
key_risks: Jumping has been consistently ponderous, which will be punished here
Lacks the 'X-factor' required for a Grade 1 victory

rankings.name: Now Is The Hour
key_positive_factors: Thyestes Chase winner with proven stamina for a slog
key_risks: Fortunate winner last time and form is well below championship standard
Needs extreme testing ground to be at his best

rankings.name: Joystick
key_positive_factors: Recent Navan winner who stayed on strongly over 3m
key_risks: Huge step up in class and likely outpaced by superior stablemates

rankings.name: Rushmount
key_positive_factors: Handicap winner at Thurles who is generally reliable
key_risks: Form is roughly 15-20lbs below what is required for a Brown Advisory

tricast_top_3.combination: Kaid D'authie > Romeo Coolio > Final Demand
Romeo Coolio > Kaid D'authie > Final Demand
Kaid D'authie > Final Demand > Romeo Coolio

pace_analysis.insight: A moderate tempo will favor proven stayers like Kaid D'authie while testing the unproven stamina of Romeo Coolio in the closing stages.
